selection_policy: Include Wikipedia cited list of unarmed African Americans killed by law enforcement (primary bulk source).
Include curated high-profile / historical police or state-violence cases corroborated across major news and memorial discourse.
Include names appearing in BOTH The Guardian The Counted (Black, 2015–16) AND Washington Post Fatal Force (Black), for dual open-dataset corroboration.
Exclude lynching, civilian killings, terrorist massacres, and non-police assassinations even if common on some Say Their Names lists.
Context phrases are brief memorial labels, not legal verdicts.

primary_sources.name: Wikipedia — List of unarmed African Americans killed by law enforcement officers in the United States
SPLC Civil Rights Martyrs (historical police/state cases)

related_not_bulk_imported.name: Mapping Police Violence
related_not_bulk_imported.note: Authoritative ongoing database; not bulk-copied here due to MPV reproduction terms. Overlap expected with WaPo/Counted/Wikipedia.

related_not_bulk_imported.name: Project Say Their Names / NON:op Say Their Names map
related_not_bulk_imported.note: Memorial projects used for cross-checks of high-profile names; not used as sole authority because some lists mix civilian killings.

intentionally_excluded_examples.name: Martin Luther King Jr. / Malcolm X / Medgar Evers
intentionally_excluded_examples.reason: Assassinations not by police as primary actor

files.core_note: Pre-2010 completeness + widely memorialized / distinctive-context cases; better default for a dignified collage than the full dual-dataset expansion.
